what_it_does: Defines a static 1600×900 HTML/CSS composition with three large plates labeled Warm, Neutral, and Cool, using design tokens and near-equal-lightness color casts to present metrics, captions, and small fragment notes.
observations: Imports Plus Jakarta Sans from Google Fonts and shared variables from /surface/styles/vars.css.
Uses a fixed-size .view canvas centered in the viewport.
Creates three grid columns for warm, neutral, and cool plates with rounded corners, light borders, and subtle shadows.
Defines --neutral from --base and --cool with color-mix(in srgb, var(--page) 62%, var(--nested)); --warm is referenced but not defined locally.
Includes decorative blueprint grid texture, circular V mark, and diagonal footer texture.
Panel content pairs color-temperature labels with metrics: 3.2×, 12, and 99.9%.
The cool panel displays the full color-mix expression as its hex label rather than a sampled color value.
standouts: The file makes hue temperature the visible variable while keeping panel lightness visually close.
The composition uses tokenized spacing, type, radius, and color throughout the CSS.
Each plate ties a color cast to a behavioral cue: holding attention, calm support, or factual reference.
conventions: Single-file HTML artifact with embedded CSS and no JavaScript.
Uses CSS custom properties heavily for palette, type, spacing, and radii.
Employs absolute positioning for the title, decorative layers, mark, and footer.
Uses semantic class names for the visual roles rather than component abstractions.
concerns: The layout is fixed at 1600×900 and has no responsive behavior.
Rendering depends on external /surface/styles/vars.css and Google Fonts.
--warm is used but not declared in this file.
The .hex label is not consistently a hex value; the cool plate shows a CSS expression.
notes: A token-driven visual specimen showing warm, neutral, and cool surface casts as equal-lightness role variants.
questions: Is --warm intentionally supplied only by the shared vars stylesheet?
Should the cool panel label show a sampled color value instead of the color-mix expression?
Is the fixed 1600×900 frame required for this artifact?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the actual file: it presents warm, neutral, and cool plates at visually similar lightness, using hue cast as the main variable.
intention_signals: It seems intended to demonstrate how warm, neutral, and cool surfaces can shape visual behavior and semantic role without relying on large lightness differences.
novelty: A three-plate comparison where hue cast carries the distinction while type, spacing, and card structure remain parallel.
contribution: Contributes a compact substrate for hue-temperature roles: warm surface emphasis, neutral middle-ground support, and cool reference context, expressed through reusable design tokens and parallel panel structure.
summary_for_embedding: Static tokenized HTML/CSS specimen presenting three near-equal-lightness warm, neutral, and cool plates with metrics and behavioral color-role notes.
intention_for_embedding: Built to show how hue temperature can differentiate attention, support, and reference roles within a consistent surface system.
